title: cota oxylepis
intro: Cota oxylepis, also known as Oxylepis scotica, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is primarily found in the northern hemisphere, specifically in the UK, and has been used for its medicinal properties for centuries. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and digestive issues. Cota oxylepis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to aid in the relief of symptoms associated with colds, flu, and other infections.
